content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

All three proposed amendments address concrete correctness and safety regressions in stale daemon/task state handling; I recommend accepting them as-is.

## Votes

- `fr-7-19abe56a`: Accept
  The cleanup path passes the per-repo daemon state directory as base_dir, but PID resolution is relative to base_dir/.git, so it can miss the actual checkout pid path in multi-repo mode. That leaves stale run.pid state behind and undermines shutdown cleanup, so the fix is warranted.
- `fr-7-45f8456b`: Accept
  Recovered-running flow currently uses only observed_running_attempt_owner, which can miss a dangling task.lease_id when run.pid/writer lock are gone. This can reopen a recovery lease despite contradictory detached-owner metadata, allowing unsafe continuation of partially cleaned state; parity with failed/paused recovery fallback is required.
- `fr-7-dcb45071`: Accept
  Collapsing all read_lease() failures into orphan-clearing is too permissive. read_lease can fail on corrupt or malformed lease records and I/O errors; clearing task.lease_id in those cases hides durable state corruption instead of quarantining/requiring explicit recovery.
